mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears and force immediate equity sell-offs.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.3pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a broad-based contraction in consumer demand that suppresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a statistically significant logit coefficient (p > 0.05) over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of low inflation reflects healthy capacity utilization and robust manufacturing output, which supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth fails to generate positive 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 0.3%, or the signal Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5 in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which directly supports consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 5-day forward returns over a 4-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level exceeding 3.5% triggers financial condition tightening that increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of periods where FEDFUNDS remains above 3.5%, invalidating the valuation compression effect.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling real personal consumption expenditures indicates supply-side driven growth that fails to translate into consumer demand, causing equity markets to price in future earnings weakness.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and RSAFS growth rates yields a positive or insignificant coefficient in predicting 20-day returns over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a period of rising unemployment signals a synchronized manufacturing and labor contraction that forces equity markets to discount severe recession risks.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth fails to predict negative 5-day returns when UNRATE is rising, or the signal hit rate falls below 40% in this regime.
